name: Eltxumendi and San Gregorio Loop via Plazaola Greenway
name_original: Eltxumendi - San Gregorio – circular por la Vía Verde del Plazaola
description: This moderate 14.2-mile mountain biking route combines the smooth Plazaola Greenway with challenging mountain trails and scenic views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Iza offers diverse terrain within the serene Boyacá highlands of Colombia. The region features a mix of paved and unpaved sections, with notable inclines on "mythical ports" like Crucero Surtipan and Curies Summit. Its location near Lago de Tota and within the scenic Sugamuxi valley provides varied landscapes for exploration. Lush greenways, such as the Plazaola Greenway, are also part of the area's natural features. Best mountain bike trails around Iza The most popular mountain bike trail is Eltxumendi and San Gregorio Loop via Plazaola Greenway, a 14.2 miles (22.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 29 minutes to complete. This route incorporates sections of the Plazaola Greenway, offering varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is San Miguel de Aralar and Artxueta Loop, a difficult 22.1 miles (35.6 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, including a climb to Artxueta. Local mountain bikers also love the View from Monte Ezkaba – Ezkaba (895 m) loop from Iruñerria, a 19.1 miles (30.8 km) trail leading through Monte Ezkaba, often completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Mountain biking around Iza is defined by diverse terrain, challenging climbs on "mythical ports," and scenic greenways. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to difficult ascents. The routes in Iza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 370 reviews. More than 3,600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Iza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Iza?
A: Iza offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 230 routes available. These range from easy rides to very challenging ascents, catering to various skill levels.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Iza?
A: Yes, Iza has approximately 38 easy mountain bike trails. These routes are generally less demanding, making them suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. Look for trails with a 'moderate' or 'easy' difficulty rating.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Iza?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Iza features around 54 difficult mountain bike trails. The region is known for its 'mythical ports' like Crucero Surtipan and Curies Summit, which offer significant inclines and elevation gains. An example of a challenging route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990042306/san-miguel-de-aralar-and-artxueta-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Miguel de Aralar and Artxueta Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which includes a climb to Artxueta.

Q: What natural attractions can I explore while mountain biking in Iza?
A: The Iza region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the picturesque Sugamuxi Valley and enjoy views of Lago de Tota, Colombia's largest inland lake. For specific natural features, consider routes that pass by highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4013419" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artazul Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4107585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ixkier Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1064398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orgi Forest&lt;/a&gt; also offers a serene natural setting.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sights near the mountain bike routes in Iza?
A: Yes, Iza itself is known for its colonial architecture, including the neo-Gothic Church of the Divine Saviour and a charming central square. The region also has a rich pre-Hispanic heritage, with rock art, myths, and legends. While mountain biking, you might encounter historical landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4817230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magdalena Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes in Iza?
A: Many mountain bike routes in Iza are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990036353/eltxumendi-and-san-gregorio-loop-via-plazaola-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eltxumendi and San Gregorio Loop via Plazaola Greenway&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option, incorporating varied terrain along the Plazaola Greenway.

Q: How long are the mountain bike trails in Iza?
A: The length of mountain bike trails in Iza varies significantly. You can find shorter routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38207404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Monte Ezkaba – Ezkaba (895 m) loop from Iruñerria&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 19.1 miles (30.8 km) long. Longer and more demanding options, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38157119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plazaola Greenway – Santa Cruz Tunnel loop from Iza / Itza&lt;/a&gt;, can extend up to 45 miles (72.4 km).

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Iza?
A: The mountain bike trails around Iza are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.2 stars from over 370 reviews. More than 3,600 mountain bikers have explored the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes, challenging climbs, and scenic greenways like the Plazaola Greenway.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Iza?
A: Iza, located in the Boyacá highlands, offers a pleasant climate for outdoor activities. While specific seasonal recommendations depend on local weather patterns, the lush landscapes and natural attractions are generally enjoyable year-round. It's always advisable to check local weather forecasts before planning your ride.

Q: Are there any routes that follow greenways in Iza?
A: Yes, the Iza area features scenic greenways that are popular for mountain biking. The Plazaola Greenway is a notable example, offering pleasant sections for riding. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38151874" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gulina Viaduct – Plazaola Greenway loop from Irurtzun&lt;/a&gt; incorporate parts of this greenway, providing a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces.

Q: Can I find natural hot springs near the mountain bike trails in Iza?
A: Yes, on the outskirts of Iza town, there are natural hot spring wells. These offer a chance for relaxation and rejuvenation, making them a perfect stop after a day of mountain biking on the nearby trails.
tips: To achieve these comforting views of the north of Pamplona you will have to give it your all on the short but demanding climb no matter where you come from and which path you choose. When you get here, take a few minutes to enjoy this beautiful landscape.
An impressive view of the Pamplona basin.

This greenway has gained fame as the most beautiful in Spain and it has everything to be so. In its 60 kilometers of length you will travel through impressive landscapes, tunnels more than three kilometers long and sites of great historical and cultural interest in this beautiful community of Navarra.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a mix of terrain. The Plazaola Greenway section primarily features well-maintained gravel paths, following an old railway line with gentle gradients, numerous tunnels, and bridges. In contrast, the Eltxumendi area provides more traditional mountain biking terrain, including singletrack descents, forest paths, rocky sections, and demanding climbs through pine forests.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This circular mountain biking route typically takes around 2 hours and 29 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your pace, skill level, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable features. The route includes sections of the scenic Plazaola Greenway, known for its historic tunnels and bridges, including the impressive 2.7 km Uitzi Tunnel. You'll also pass the Eltxumendi Summit and enjoy a View of the Pamplona Basin from Monasterio de San Esteban de Juslapeña, offering spectacular vistas.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While the Plazaola Greenway sections are generally easy to moderate with gentle gradients, the Eltxumendi area includes more challenging mountain biking terrain with demanding climbs and potentially technical descents. Beginners with some experience might find it manageable, but those new to mountain biking may find certain sections difficult.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this route is generally during spring, summer, and autumn. These seasons offer more favorable weather conditions, making the trails more enjoyable and safer. Winter riding might be possible but could involve colder temperatures, potential snow or ice, and wet, muddy conditions, especially in the mountain sections.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on the Plazaola Greenway and most mountain biking trails in the Eltxumendi area. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in shared-use areas, near livestock, or in protected natural zones, to ensure their safety and the comfort of other users.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay any entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or have to pay any entrance fees to access the Plazaola Greenway or the Eltxumendi mountain biking trails. They are publicly accessible.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details for the exact starting point of this circular route are not provided, but access points to the Plazaola Greenway are available in various villages along its length, such as Lekunberri. For the Eltxumendi area, parking can typically be found near trailheads or in nearby towns like Oteiza de Berrioplano, which offer convenient access to the mountain trails.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other trails. It extensively uses the Plazaolako bide berdea and the Vía Verde del Plazaola. You will also find parts of the Asturian Way and the Tour of the Pamplona Basin along the route.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while riding?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse and beautiful scenery. The Plazaola Greenway takes you through lush green landscapes, dense forests (including beech and oak groves), picturesque valleys, and charming villages. The Eltxumendi area offers spectacular views from higher elevations, overlooking varied landscapes, including the Pamplona Basin.

faqs.question: Are there any particularly steep sections on the route?
faqs.answer: While the Plazaola Greenway is mostly gentle, there can be sections where it deviates from the original railway line that present quite hard, steep slopes, sometimes exceeding 10%. The Eltxumendi area also features demanding climbs and hard pedaling sections, especially when ascending towards the summit.

waypoints.name: View of the Pamplona Basin from Monasterio de San Esteban de Juslapeña
